ops.body:
**E1 feasibility spike complete (2026-07-19): GO — scoped as a future protocol-breaking transport project.**

Two findings, both grounded in evidence:

1. **The purego -> Security.framework verifier is ALREADY implemented and shipping under CGO_ENABLED=0.** Not a hypothesis: wire/peer_darwin.go:22-34 (purego dlopen libSystem, dlsym getsockopt), wire/peer_darwin.go:60-82 (getsockopt LOCAL_PEERTOKEN -> audit_token_t), trust/verify_darwin.go:69-248 (dlopen CoreFoundation+Security; SecCodeCopyGuestWithAttributes keyed on kSecGuestAttributeAudit; SecCodeCheckValidityWithErrors). `task build` (= CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./...) exits 0; only deps are purego v0.10.1 + x/sys. The DR-verification half of any XPC transport is reusable as-is.

2. **LOCAL_PEERTOKEN is query-time-LIVE, not connection-bound — no cheap fix exists.** Confirmed two ways: (a) XNU source — bsd/kern/uipc_usrreq.c uipc_ctloutput LOCAL_PEERTOKEN case does proc_find(peerso->last_pid)+task_info(TASK_AUDIT_TOKEN) on EVERY call, no cached unp_peertoken field; last_pid = "most recent accessor", rewritten by so_update_last_owner_locked on essentially every socket I/O. (b) Empirical: a pure-Go CGO-off demo where a child connects, the server reads its code identity, then the child execve("/bin/sleep")s on the SAME pid + SAME connection -> server derives a DIFFERENT identity (TF1 substitution demonstrated live, deterministic over 4 runs). Moving trust after the frame read (ae67609 item 1) only narrows the window; it does not close it.

**Consequence for E4:** on the unix-socket transport, TF1 (fork/exec substitution), TF2 (PID reuse), and TF5 (fd delegation / SCM_RIGHTS / setuid) CANNOT become passing rejection tests — they are documented-limitation tests (assert substitution SUCCEEDS, codifying the known-unsound boundary). They become true rejection tests only against an XPC transport.

**Recommended transport (future project, not this session):** an xpc package using the PUBLIC, kernel-enforced `xpc_connection_set_peer_code_signing_requirement` (macOS 12+; structured team/entitlement peer-requirement API macOS 26+, matching CI's macos-26 job). Hand it daemonkit's existing DR string; the kernel enforces per-connection/message. No new dependency (purego.NewCallback covers the block-ABI handler primitive — the one real engineering risk, prototype-able early). It BREAKS the frozen wire protocol (xpc_dictionary vs length-prefixed JSON), so it is a transport swap requiring a consumer-migration / parallel-transport story across wire/client.go, lifeproto, fusekit-holder, cc-pool, synckit. Scope as a full Wave-4 transport project.

GO to build it; verifier half done, transport half is the substantial work. (Note: xpc_dictionary_get_audit_token is SPI/private — the CSR API is the correct public route.)
